description: A modular, freedom-first operating system ecosystem built ground-up in Rust, inspired by Egyptian mythology. Targets mobile, laptop, and desktop with a unified codebase and two release philosophies.
philosophy: Everything ground up. Every name earns its place. Every component knows its job. Nothing overlaps.

mobile.name: Osiris Ramesses
mobile.audience: General users — people tired of Google/Apple but not willing to tinker
mobile.philosophy: Stable, prosperous, just works. Sane defaults. Services managed silently.

mobile.name: Osiris Netjeru
mobile.philosophy: Exposed, divine, no guardrails. Every daemon visible and replaceable.

desktop.name: Osiris OS
desktop.note: Built first. Mobile follows after PC stack is stable.

phase_1a.name: Pinephone Pro
phase_1a.reason: Best open mobile Linux target. Mainline kernel, open bootloader, hardware kill switches, community aligned with Osiris.

phase_1b.name: Framework Laptop
phase_1b.reason: Shared modularity philosophy. Excellent mainline Linux support. Target audience overlap with Netjeru users. Ra desktop battle-tested here before mobile.

phase_2.name: Sokar
phase_2.description: Ground-up hardware designed for Osiris. Modular components mirroring OPIUM package philosophy. Hardware kill switches mapped per daemon. Open bootloader by design.
phase_2.mythology: Sokar — Egyptian god of the physical vessel, craftsman of sacred objects. Later merged with Osiris into Ptah-Sokar-Osiris.

init_system.name: Kha
init_system.description: Minimal Rust binary. Mounts essential filesystems, reaps zombies, forwards signals. Single responsibility: stay alive and hand control to AkerNet Bridge.
init_system.mythology: Kha — Egyptian concept of the life-force/spirit double.

the_abyss_network.description: 42 daemons across 9 domains. The foundation everything runs on. Unseen, modular, everything depends on it.
the_abyss_network.mythology: The Abyss (Nun) — primordial void everything emerged from. The 42 Assessors of Ma'at — divine judges at the weighing of the soul. The OS declares itself to 42 daemons before it lives.

daemons.name: gpiod
daemons.responsibility: Hardware kill switch states, GPIO events

daemons.name: inputd
daemons.responsibility: Unified input via libinput. Keyboard, mouse, touch, stylus.

daemons.name: usbd
daemons.responsibility: USB OTG, hotplug, gadget mode switching

daemons.name: displayd
daemons.responsibility: Output management, external display routing, convergence trigger

daemons.name: voiced
daemons.responsibility: Call audio, noise cancellation, mic routing

akernet.name: AkerNet
akernet.description: Command/control abstraction layer. Spawned by Kha. Orchestrates all 42 daemons via dependency graph. Owns the network domain entirely.

subcomponents.akernet_bridge: Rust/Axum HTTP bridge. Daemon lifecycle management.
subcomponents.aker_app: Kotlin Android app. Unified control surface for all daemons. SDK 34.

akernet.mythology: Aker — Egyptian god of the earth and horizon, guardian of transitions.

ra.name: Ra

modes.desktop: Floating windows, panel, keyboard shortcuts. Default on Framework/desktop.
modes.mobile: Fullscreen app management, gesture navigation, status bar.
modes.convergence: Auto-switches on external display + peripheral detection via convergenced daemon.

ra.description: The compositor and visual shell of Osiris. One codebase across phone, laptop, and desktop.
ra.mythology: Ra — Egyptian sun god. The light of Osiris.

opium.name: OPIUM
opium.description: High-level package manager. Resolves dependencies, manages repos, handles upgrades. Calls Harvester underneath.

harvester.name: Harvester
harvester.description: Low-level package handler. Installs raw .osr packages, maintains package database. No dependency resolution — that's OPIUM's job.
harvester.mobile_role: APK extractor from existing Android installs + Waydroid bridge for running extracted apps inside Osiris.
harvester.pc_role: dpkg-equivalent. Touches the filesystem directly. OPIUM calls it.

package_format.name: Osiris Package
package_format.description: Native Osiris package format. Every .osr carries the Osiris name. Managed by Harvester, orchestrated by OPIUM.

anubis.name: Anubis
anubis.description: System management UI. Manages processes, storage, users, system health. Built after the core stack is stable.
anubis.mythology: Anubis — Egyptian god of the dead, guardian and judge of souls.

thoth.name: Thoth
thoth.description: Native Osiris text editor. Built inside Osiris, for Osiris.
thoth.mythology: Thoth — Egyptian god of writing, knowledge, and scribes.

heka.name: Heka
heka.description: Native Osiris IDE. Existing Tauri codebase. Built inside Osiris.
heka.mythology: Heka — Egyptian god of magic and medicine.
heka.status: Legacy IDE intended to become a built-in Osiris application; source/import path still needs to be recorded.

repoSpace.name: RepoSpace
repoSpace.description: Osiris-native IDE with integrated repo management. The inhouse GitHub of Osiris.
repoSpace.status: Built previously according to project history; reconcile with Heka naming/product boundary before adding to workspace.
repoSpace.note: Simple yet better than VS Code.

build_environment.primary: Samsung Galaxy A07 — Termux + proot-distro (Debian Trixie)
build_environment.bare_termux_role: Supervisor layer. AkerNet bridge, Git operations, backup scripts.
build_environment.proot_debian_role: Construction site. Full build environment. Osiris system lives here.
build_environment.phantom_process_killer: Must be disabled in Developer Options before any build session.
build_environment.wake_lock: Termux wake lock must be acquired.

layer_1.name: GitHub monorepo
layer_1.description: Granular versioned history. One repo, all components.

layer_2.name: Local proot snapshot
layer_2.description: tar snapshot of proot Debian after each stage.

layer_3.name: Android storage
layer_3.description: Mirror of Layer 2 snapshots on /sdcard/osiris-backups. Survives Debian death entirely.

build_roadmap.note: PC first. Mobile follows after PC stack is stable.

milestone_0.name: Hosted Osiris Core
milestone_0.purpose: Establish a runnable Osiris core inside Termux/proot before claiming real OS boot or hardware support.
tasks: Kha runs as a normal hosted process and can supervise akernet-bridge
AkerNet bridge exposes root and /health endpoints from the reconciled Netjeru implementation
Harvester can harvest, install, remove, and list a simple .osr package in the hosted environment
OPIUM can call Harvester and maintain package metadata
Document host-provided vs Osiris-provided responsibilities for Termux/proot, Debian scaffold, and future hardware boot
Record import plan for external ra, netjeru, and Heka/RepoSpace projects

stage_1.name: Foundation
tasks: Minimal Debian base confirmed in proot
AkerNet bridge ported into Osiris monorepo
Kha skeleton — PID 1 stub, boots to shell
Core Abyss daemons: logd, mountd, healthd, timed

stage_3.name: Display + Session
tasks: Ra roadmap completion: resize, focus highlight, wallpaper

stage_4.name: Complete the Stack
tasks: Thoth — text editor, built inside Osiris

stage_5.name: Mobile Port
tasks: Ra-mobile — ra-shell layer, touch input, gesture navigation
Convergence mode — auto-switch on peripheral detection

stage_6.name: Framework Laptop
tasks: Ra desktop battle-tested on real laptop

naming_mythology.Osiris: The god of resurrection and the afterlife. The OS itself.
naming_mythology.Kha: The life-force/spirit double. Init — keeps everything alive.
naming_mythology.Ra: The sun god. The compositor — the light of Osiris.
naming_mythology.AkerNet: Aker, guardian of transitions. The network and orchestration layer.
naming_mythology.Thoth: God of writing and knowledge. The text editor.
naming_mythology.Anubis: Guardian and judge of souls. The system manager.
naming_mythology.Harvester: The reaper — takes what is needed, installs it cleanly.
naming_mythology.The Abyss Network: Nun, the primordial void. The 42 daemons — the 42 Assessors of Ma'at.
naming_mythology.Ramesses: Ramesses II — the most prosperous pharaoh. The daily driver release.
naming_mythology.Netjeru: The gods/divine beings. The power user release.
naming_mythology.Sokar: God of the physical vessel. The Osiris reference hardware.
naming_mythology.RepoSpace: The space where Osiris builds itself.

current_status.summary: Osiris is a Rust workspace with core crates scaffolded. Kha, OPIUM, and Harvester have meaningful hosted/proot-era code. AkerNet and Ra have older source projects in ~/projects that must be reconciled before roadmap status can be trusted. Heka/RepoSpace is planned as a built-in first-party application but is not currently present in this workspace.

implementation_snapshot.kha: Implemented as a hosted/proot-friendly supervisor that mounts essentials when possible, spawns akernet-bridge, reaps child processes, and restarts the bridge. Not yet true boot PID 1 on hardware.
implementation_snapshot.akernet: Osiris monorepo copy is currently a Stage 2 stub. Original Netjeru akernet-bridge has an Axum HTTP server with root and /health routes on port 7474.
implementation_snapshot.ra: Osiris monorepo ra-wm is currently a placeholder for v0.8.0-alpha Wayland rebuild. External ~/projects/ra contains an older X11/x11rb window manager with resize, framing, config, and panel work.
implementation_snapshot.opium: CLI package manager exists with install/remove/purge/list/search/info/update/harvest commands and delegates harvesting to Harvester.
implementation_snapshot.harvester: CLI workhorse exists with harvest/install/remove/list/env commands and environment-aware config.
implementation_snapshot.anubis: Workspace member present; implementation status not reviewed in this pass.
implementation_snapshot.heka_repoSpace: Legacy IDE exists by project history and should become a built-in Osiris application; source location/import plan still needs to be recorded.

ra.status: External source to reconcile with osiris/ra before Stage 3.

netjeru.status: External source to reconcile with osiris/akernet before Stage 2. Contains embedded AdGuardHome data that should not be blindly imported into the monorepo.

heka_repoSpace.role: Legacy IDE / repo hub intended as built-in Osiris app
heka_repoSpace.status: Find source, decide whether Heka and RepoSpace are one product lineage or separate apps, then import as a workspace member.

immediate_risks: No local README or status ledger, so project memory depends on roadmap accuracy.
Git status appears to come from a parent repository rather than an Osiris-local repo. Confirm repository boundaries before commits.
Several roadmap components exist both as stubs in Osiris and as older external projects; migration policy is needed to avoid losing working code.

notes: Workspace checks successfully in dev profile.
opium currently emits unused/dead-code warnings in repo.rs and manifest.rs; no hard build failures.
